top_signals.title: The World Cup: Players Vie for Goals and Countries for Influence
top_signals.summary:
The 2026 FIFA Men’s World Cup is now well underway, bringing 48 countries’ teams to compete in a soccer tournament co-hosted by the United States, Mexico, and Canada. With teams from every continent except Antarctica, the 2026 tournament is a truly global event. The World Cup is often hailed as an opportunity for the world to come together and celebrate a shared passion for “the beautiful game” — creating an opportunity every four years for countries to gain attention, shape their reputation, and build influence.Although the joint U.S.-Mexican-Canadian bid intended to demonstrate North American connections, relations have deteriorated since the

The post The World Cup: Players Vie for Goals and Countries for Influence appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: BeHydro Receives Lloyd’s Register First Class Approval for Hydrogen Marine Engine
top_signals.summary: Lloyd’s Register (LR) has issued the first Type Approval Certificate for a 100% hydrogen-fueled, spark-ignited marine engine.The approval has been awarded to the hydrogen engine developed by BeHydro and confirms the design meets LR’s requirements for safety…

top_signals.title: Bitcoin traders expect new lows but data cautions against overly bearish bias
top_signals.summary: A concentrated liquidity pocket below $59,000 raises the chance of a Bitcoin price sell-off to new 2026 lows, but data suggests bulls will absorb the dip.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
